reasoning: The US collected ~$82 billion in customs duties in FY 2024. For this to jump to $500 billion or more in FY 2025, it would require a massively unprecedented, immediate implementation of universal tariffs by the incoming Trump administration. Given that FY 2025 started in October 2024, Trump wouldn't take office until nearly 4 months into the fiscal year. Even if an extreme universal tariff (e.g., 20% on all goods) were imposed shortly after taking office, the implementation timeline and the expected drop in import volumes make reaching $500 billion in customs duties collections by September 30, 2025 functionally impossible. The market probability is 0.2%, and strictly due to the 0.02 bounds, I will predict 0.02.
